info.name: Vess Device Service
info.description:
Complete collection for the Vess Device Management Service API.

**Setup:**
1. Set `baseUrl` to your deployment URL.
2. Set `accessToken` to a valid JWT from the auth-service (or import the auth collection and run Login first — the token is shared via the `accessToken` variable if both collections share an environment).
3. After registering a device, the `deviceId` and `apiKey` variables are auto-populated for use in heartbeat and location requests.

**Auth types used:**
- `UserJWTAuthentication` — `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` for dashboard/user-facing endpoints.
- `DeviceAPIKeyAuthentication` — `X-API-Key: <apiKey>` for device-originated endpoints (heartbeat, location).
- Registration endpoints use a dashboard JWT decoded with `SECRET_KEY` (same Bearer token).

request.description: Liveness probe. Returns service health including database and cache status.

request.description: Deep system health check across all dependencies.

originalRequest.description: Deep system health check across all dependencies.

originalRequest.description: Swagger UI for the device service API.

query.description: Filter by status: ONLINE | OFFLINE | STALE | TESTING | DEGRADED
Case-insensitive substring match on location field

request.description: List devices with optional filters. **Scoping rules:** admins and service tokens see all devices; operators and viewers see only devices they registered (where `owner_id` matches their JWT `user_id`).

query.description: Filter by status: ONLINE | OFFLINE | STALE | TESTING | DEGRADED
Case-insensitive substring match on location field

originalRequest.description: Returns a paginated list of all devices. Supports filtering by status, location, and date range.

request.description: Return status for each provided device ID. Any unknown ID is returned with found=false and error=not_found. Supports dashboard and service Bearer tokens.

exec: console.log('API Key (save this — it is shown only once):', body.api_key);

request.description: Register a new device. Returns a one-time `api_key` — store it immediately as it cannot be retrieved again. Coordinates must be within Nigeria bounds (lat 4–14°N, lng 3–15°E). The `owner_id` is automatically set from the caller's JWT `user_id`. Non-admin users (operator/viewer) can only access their own devices after registration. The `deviceId` and `apiKey` variables are auto-saved.

originalRequest.description: Register a new device. Returns a one-time `api_key` — store it immediately as it cannot be retrieved again. Coordinates must be within Nigeria bounds (lat 4–14°N, lng 3–15°E). The `deviceId` and `apiKey` variables are auto-saved.

request.description: Retrieve full device details. Non-admin users can only access their own devices — attempting to access another user’s device returns 404.

response.name: Example Success

originalRequest.description: Retrieve full device details including health metrics, recent test executions (from test-service), and aggregated statistics.

request.description: Update device configuration or metadata. Requires `admin` or `operator` role in the JWT. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated. Metadata is merged (not replaced).

originalRequest.description: Update device configuration or metadata. Requires `admin` or `operator` role in the JWT. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated. Metadata is merged (not replaced).

request.description: Soft-deregisters a device by setting `is_active=false` and recording `deregistered_at`. Requires `admin` role in the JWT. Publishes a `device.deregistered` event.

originalRequest.description: Soft-deregisters a device by setting `is_active=false` and recording `deregistered_at`. Requires `admin` role in the JWT. Publishes a `device.deregistered` event.

query.description: Required. Searches across device_id, device_name, and location fields.

request.description: Full-text search across `device_id`, `device_name`, and `location`. Returns up to 50 results ordered by `created_at` descending.

query.description: Required. Searches across device_id, device_name, and location fields.

originalRequest.description: Full-text search across `device_id`, `device_name`, and `location`. Returns up to 50 results ordered by `created_at` descending.

query.description: Optional. Search radius in kilometres. Defaults to 50.

request.description: Geospatial search using the Haversine formula. Returns all devices within `radius_km` of the given coordinates, sorted by distance ascending. Each result includes a `distance_km` field.

query.description: Optional. Search radius in kilometres. Defaults to 50.

originalRequest.description: Geospatial search using the Haversine formula. Returns all devices within `radius_km` of the given coordinates, sorted by distance ascending. Each result includes a `distance_km` field.

  "network_type": "4G",
  "network_operator": "MTN Nigeria",
  "signal_strength": -75,
  "os_version": "13",
  "app_version": "2.4.1",

  "longitude": 3.3947,
  "location": "Lagos Island, Lagos",
  "physical_address": "Plot 123 Ikoyi, Lagos"
}

request.description:
Device-originated endpoint. Authenticated via `X-API-Key` header (not Bearer JWT).

Updates device status based on health metrics:
- `battery_level < 15` → DEGRADED (low_battery)
- `signal_strength < -100` → DEGRADED (low_signal)
- `storage_available_mb < 500` → DEGRADED (low_storage)
- `network_type == '2G'` → DEGRADED (poor_network)
- Otherwise → ONLINE
- TESTING status is preserved regardless of metrics.

Returns `pending_commands` count and `next_heartbeat_in_ms`.

originalRequest.description:
Device-originated endpoint. Authenticated via `X-API-Key` header (not Bearer JWT).

Updates device status based on health metrics:
- `battery_level < 15` → DEGRADED (low_battery)
- `signal_strength < -100` → DEGRADED (low_signal)
- `storage_available_mb < 500` → DEGRADED (low_storage)
- `network_type == '2G'` → DEGRADED (poor_network)
- Otherwise → ONLINE
- TESTING status is preserved regardless of metrics.

Returns `pending_commands` count and `next_heartbeat_in_ms`.

request.description: Create a single device registration request. Generates a one-time sync URL sent via SMS to the field agent's phone number. The `registrationRequestId` and `syncToken` variables are auto-saved from the response.

originalRequest.description: Create a single device registration request. Generates a one-time sync URL sent via SMS to the field agent's phone number. The `registrationRequestId` and `syncToken` variables are auto-saved from the response.

request.description: Create up to 500 registration requests in a single call. Each entry gets its own sync URL and SMS. Returns `count` and `results` array.

originalRequest.description: Create up to 500 registration requests in a single call. Each entry gets its own sync URL and SMS. Returns `count` and `results` array.

request.description: Retrieve details of a specific registration request. Only the user who created the request can access it.

originalRequest.description: Retrieve details of a specific registration request. Only the user who created the request can access it.

request.description: Revoke a pending registration sync link by marking it as expired. Returns 409 if the request has already been approved.

originalRequest.description: Revoke a pending registration sync link by marking it as expired. Returns 409 if the request has already been approved.

request.description: Generate a new sync link for an existing registration request (e.g. if the previous link expired). Resets status to `pending` and issues a new SMS. Returns 409 if already approved. The new `syncToken` is auto-saved.

originalRequest.description: Generate a new sync link for an existing registration request (e.g. if the previous link expired). Resets status to `pending` and issues a new SMS. Returns 409 if already approved. The new `syncToken` is auto-saved.

request.description:
Called by the device app when the field agent opens the sync URL. Validates the token and returns registration metadata (phone number, reference, expiry). Returns 404 for invalid tokens, 410 for used or expired ones.

Rate-limited (validate throttle).

originalRequest.description:
Called by the device app when the field agent opens the sync URL. Validates the token and returns registration metadata (phone number, reference, expiry). Returns 404 for invalid tokens, 410 for used or expired ones.

Rate-limited (validate throttle).

exec: console.log('Device approved via sync. device_id:', body.device_id);
console.log('API Key (save this — shown only once):', body.api_key);

request.description:
Called by the device app to complete registration via the sync link. Atomically creates the device record, marks the registration request as `approved`, and returns a one-time `api_key`. The sync link is consumed and cannot be reused.

Rate-limited (complete throttle). `deviceId` and `apiKey` are auto-saved.

originalRequest.description:
Called by the device app to complete registration via the sync link. Atomically creates the device record, marks the registration request as `approved`, and returns a one-time `api_key`. The sync link is consumed and cannot be reused.

Rate-limited (complete throttle). `deviceId` and `apiKey` are auto-saved.

variable.description: JWT Bearer token from auth-service login. Used for all user-facing endpoints.
One-time API key returned on device registration. Used for heartbeat and location endpoints.
Auto-populated after creating a registration request.
Sync link token extracted from sync_url after creating a registration request.
